role: A small, stateless React control that surfaces the current relative centre and offers a one-tap reset back to the root origin.
capabilities: Display active centre label when centre is non-null
Hide itself entirely when centred on root (null)
Provide a reset action via native button click
Expose accessible name/title describing current state and reset action
Apply an address stamp for tracing/testing
substance_why: The component fully implements its stated purpose with real props, motion, accessibility text, and a reset callback, leaving no TODOs or stubs.
interactions_present: Animated appear/disappear driven by centre state changes
interactions_missing: No disabled or loading state while reset is in flight
No visible focus/hover affordance defined in code
No truncation or overflow handling for long centre labels
No keyboard shortcut or gesture for reset
No confirmation/undo for returning to root
performance_risks: Framer Motion AnimatePresence runs enter/exit animations on every centre toggle; rapid changes can queue motion work
Component is not memoized, so parent re-renders recreate motion props and re-render the chip
stamp() and transition objects are spread inline on every render, adding minor per-render object churn
reuse_why: The behaviour and concept are focused and reusable, but the markup, motion tokens, and address stamping should be reconciled with the new design system.
decomposition_hints: Extract a generic animated chip/pill wrapper if similar controls appear elsewhere
Separate the label formatting and reset affordance into presentational subcomponents
Move address stamping into a shared hook or design-system primitive instead of inline spread
